metadata.title: Tier 1 plugin-security sub-rubric
metadata.description: Security-review rules calibrated for Claude Code plugin projects. Activates only when project-config.yaml declares project_kind: claude-code-plugin. Layer 2 (sub-rubric) of the four-layer rubric composition (base + sub + regime + project). Owns the allowed-tools claims-vs-usage drift check and the requires_adapter: shellcheck placeholder that defers shell-script linting to Tier 2.
metadata.requires_adapter_advisory: Shell scripts detected — shellcheck validation deferred to Tier 2

requires_adapter_probe.description: The advisory helper (scripts/plugin-shellcheck-advisory.sh) consults tool-availability-probe.sh in tri-state mode to classify the shellcheck adapter as omitted, null, or declared. omitted/null with .sh files present -> emit advisory; declared -> delegate to the shellcheck adapter. Never use a hardcoded path.

allowed_tools_drift.description: Static check that compares each SKILL.md frontmatter `allowed-tools:` declaration against the actual tool invocations referenced in the skill body and any scripts the skill calls. Drift (a tool used but not declared) MUST emit a CRITICAL finding — privilege-escalation risk in plugin skills must not be downgraded.

severity_rules.pattern: A SKILL.md file in the plugin tree invokes a tool (Bash, Read, Write, Edit, Grep, Glob, Agent, WebFetch, WebSearch, Task) that is not declared in its frontmatter `allowed-tools:` list. Detected by scripts/allowed-tools-drift-check.sh which parses the SKILL.md frontmatter, scans the skill body and any referenced scripts, and reports each undeclared tool invocation.
severity_rules.description: Plugin skills that invoke tools not declared in `allowed-tools:` create a privilege-escalation surface. The Claude Code harness only enforces allowed-tools at the skill boundary — a skill that quietly grows new tool usage past its declared surface can elevate the plugin's effective capabilities without review. Drift MUST emit CRITICAL — no downgrade, no allowlist.
severity_rules.remediation: Either remove the undeclared tool invocation from the skill (and its referenced scripts) or update the SKILL.md frontmatter `allowed-tools:` list to include the tool, then have a security reviewer confirm the new capability is intentional.

severity_rules.pattern: The plugin tree contains one or more `.sh` files AND the shellcheck adapter is absent from project-config.yaml `tool_adapters:` (tri-state probe returns `omitted` or `null`). The advisory helper (scripts/plugin-shellcheck-advisory.sh) emits the canonical advisory text "Shell scripts detected — shellcheck validation deferred to Tier 2" and exits 0 — the rubric does NOT fail.
severity_rules.description: Tier 1 plugin installs ship without the shellcheck adapter. Rather than silently skip shell-script validation (which would hide real defects) or hard-fail the rubric (which would block adoption), this rule emits a visible advisory pointing to Tier 2 as the upgrade path. The advisory text is a stable contract string — do not paraphrase it.
severity_rules.remediation: To enable shellcheck validation, install the shellcheck adapter and declare it under `tool_adapters:` in project-config.yaml. The advisory will stop firing and the rubric will delegate shell-script linting to the adapter automatically.
